landing.hero.headline: hicode makes Claude Code and Codex ready to use in your browser.
landing.hero.subheadline: Start Claude Code or Codex in a browser terminal with cloud workspaces, file edits, live previews, model routing, and resumable sessions.
landing.features.title: Claude Code and Codex, ready in the browser
landing.features.description: Use real terminal-based agents with cloud runtimes, previews, archives, and managed model routing.
landing.features.auth.title: Authentication
landing.features.auth.description: Email/password, Google, and GitHub OAuth — ready out of the box with session management and account linking.
landing.features.payment.title: Payments
landing.features.payment.description: Stripe, PayPal, Alipay, WeChat Pay — checkout, subscriptions, webhooks, and order management.
landing.features.rbac.title: Roles & Permissions
landing.features.rbac.description: Fine-grained RBAC with wildcard matching. Full admin panel for managing users, roles, and permissions.
landing.features.i18n.title: Internationalization
landing.features.i18n.description: English and Chinese built-in with locale-aware routing. Easy to extend with additional languages.
landing.features.cms.title: Content Management
landing.features.cms.description: Categories and posts with full CRUD, status management, and admin panel — build your blog or docs.
landing.features.credits.title: Credit System
landing.features.credits.description: FIFO consumption, expiration, auto-grant on signup, and revocation — ready for usage-based billing.
landing.pricing.title: Pricing
landing.pricing.description: Plans grant monthly credits for sandbox runtime and platform model usage. Top-ups and BYOK can be added later.
landing.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
landing.faq.description: Everything you need to know before getting started.
landing.faq.stack.question: What's the tech stack?
landing.faq.stack.answer: Next.js 16 with the App Router, React 19, TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Drizzle ORM, and better-auth. Every piece of business logic is inlined — no external packages for payment, email, storage, or AI.
landing.faq.payment.question: Which payment providers are supported?
landing.faq.payment.answer: Stripe, PayPal, Creem, Alipay, and WeChat Pay out of the box. The PaymentManager abstraction makes it straightforward to add more providers by implementing a single interface.
landing.faq.database.question: Which databases can I use?
landing.faq.database.answer: Drizzle ORM handles PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, Turso, and Cloudflare D1. Switch providers by editing DATABASE_PROVIDER in your .env — the schema templates and drizzle config adapt automatically.
landing.faq.customize.question: How customizable is the landing page?
landing.faq.customize.answer: The src/blocks/ directory ships with demo sections — they're designed to be rewritten per project. The durable primitives live in src/components/, so your rebrand touches blocks and translations, not the chassis.
landing.faq.license.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
landing.faq.license.answer: hicode is being built as a production SaaS using the included auth, billing, credits, settings, and admin foundations.
landing.cta.headline: Open a coding workspace in seconds.
landing.cta.subheadline: Choose Claude Code or Codex, describe what you want to build, and let hicode work inside a live cloud workspace from your browser.
landing.footer.tagline: Claude Code and Codex, online in your browser.
blog.title: Blog
blog.description: Product updates, guides, and engineering notes from the team.
blog.no_posts: No posts yet — check back soon.
landing.blog.title: Engineering notes
landing.blog.description: Architecture updates and build notes from the hicode team.
landing.hero.preview_command: Create a project, run tests, and open a preview.
landing.hero.preview_output: Agent: I patched the route, verified the build, and opened the preview.
landing.features.cli.title: Real coding CLIs online
landing.features.cli.description: Run Claude Code and Codex CLI in a live terminal instead of a simplified chat clone.
landing.features.sandbox.title: Session-isolated cloud sandboxes
landing.features.sandbox.description: Each session gets an isolated Linux runtime with Node, git, package managers, tmux, and a real shell.
landing.features.preview.title: Terminal plus live previews
landing.features.preview.description: Stream terminal bytes to the browser while exposing app previews through secure session-scoped routes.
landing.features.archive.title: Resumable workspaces
landing.features.archive.description: Archive workspace files and session state to object storage, then restore them when the user returns.
landing.features.gateway.title: Managed model gateway
landing.features.gateway.description: Route model calls through a platform gateway for key protection, configurable model choices, limits, and audit trails.
landing.features.billing.title: Credits and BYOK
landing.features.billing.description: Use one clear credit balance for sandbox runtime and platform model usage, with BYOK for custom model keys.
landing.pricing.feature_byok: BYOK supported with your own model API key
landing.pricing.feature_persist: Workspace files and session history persist
landing.pricing.feature_long_sessions: More room for long-running coding sessions
landing.faq.code.question: Is hicode real coding automation online?
landing.faq.code.answer: Yes. hicode runs real terminal-based agents inside cloud runtimes, so they can read files, edit code, execute commands, run tests, and recover from errors.
landing.faq.sandbox.question: Where does a hicode session run?
landing.faq.sandbox.answer: Each new session runs in its own isolated cloud runtime namespace with a shell, tmux session, workspace directory, and archive path for recovery.
landing.faq.install.question: Do I need to install local tools?
landing.faq.install.answer: No. The browser handles the interface, while the cloud runtime provides the shell, dependencies, preview server, and persistent workspace archive.
landing.faq.billing.question: How is it billed?
landing.faq.billing.answer: Plans include monthly credits. Credits are designed to cover cloud runtime and platform model usage. BYOK can reduce model charges when you use your own upstream key.
landing.faq.privacy.question: Is my code private?
landing.faq.privacy.answer: The production design keeps model keys out of user-readable environments, isolates session runtime work, and stores workspace archives in private object storage under session-scoped paths.
code.sessions.title: Sessions
code.sessions.subtitle: Cloud workspaces
code.sessions.new_confirm_title: How should this session be handled?
code.sessions.new_confirm_description: Before starting a new session, choose what to do with the current running session.
code.sessions.new_confirm_save_descripti: The current workspace will be archived to R2 and suspended, so you can restore it later from saved sessions.
code.sessions.new_confirm_discard_descri: The current runtime will be cleared and this session will not appear in saved sessions, so it cannot be restored from the page.
code.sessions.restore_confirm_descriptio: The current active session will be archived and suspended first, then the selected workspace will be restored from R2.
code.model.configure_required: No enabled model for this agent. Ask an admin to configure one.
code.billing.insufficient_title: Not enough credits to start a session
code.billing.insufficient_description: Your balance is {balance} credits. Starting a session requires at least {required} credits.
code.billing.model_unavailable_descripti: The administrator has not completed this model's billing configuration. Choose another model or contact the administrator.
code.billing.suspended_title: Session paused because credits ran out
code.billing.suspended_description: Your workspace has been archived safely. Recharge credits, then restore this saved session to continue.
code.runtime.title: Runtime
code.runtime.lost: Session runtime was lost. Restore from the R2 archive or start a new session.
code.terminal.title: hicode terminal
code.actions.cleanup_warning: Previous session cleanup failed; new session started
code.archive.verified: Restore verified. The workspace matches the R2 archive.
code.archive.unavailable: Not saved to R2 yet. Automatic archives run after the terminal connects, or you can archive manually.
code.diff.soon: Structured diff is coming in a later release.
code.diff.title: File changes
code.diff.subtitle: Agent edits are tracked before the next preview or archive checkpoint.
code.preview.title: Live preview
code.preview.subtitle: Session-scoped preview output appears beside the terminal.
code.preview.empty: Start a session to open the live preview.
code.archive.title: Archive
code.archive.subtitle: Workspace files can be persisted and restored across runtime restarts.
